headline: The foundation flipped from absent to complete.
standfirst: Three months in, visibility moved 3.5 → 4.6 while authority held steady at 25. The headline story is in the technical layer: every measurable layer below the surface — hreflang, schema, redirects, disavow — flipped from absent to complete during May and June.

feature_chart.title: Visibility score over six months
feature_chart.subtitle: Semrush position-tracking score, UAE database
feature_chart.caption: The flat stretch from March to early May was deliberate — we were repairing the foundation before adding signal. Once hreflang and enriched Organization schema went live on June 1, Google had a clean structure to read into the index. The jump from 3.53 to 4.55 in a single measurement window is the result.

prose.title: What moved, and why.
paragraphs: Two months of work landed on June 1: hreflang annotations across all three language installs, an enriched Organization schema graph carrying three regulatory licenses, and a comprehensive disavow filing covering 586 spam domains. The Semrush visibility line responded within days.

Authority Score holding flat is expected. Semrush rebuilds Authority Score on a roughly 90-day rolling window — the disavow and 301 recovery work just landed, so the reweighting will appear later in the summer.
The next inflection should come from the Core Web Vitals work. Mobile is currently at 0% passing. Once JS/CSS optimization lands in Month 3, expect another step on the visibility chart.

pull_quote.text: Every measurable layer below the surface flipped from absent to complete in a single window.
